Playfair Display for editorial weight and authority. DM Sans for clarity and legibility. DM Mono for data and labels.

The FARM-REC system processes underutilized biomass through demineralization, microalgae-assisted fermentation, and pyrolysis to produce ruminant feed, soil amendments, and energy products.

03 — Icon Library

Illustrative Icons

17 hand-drawn woodcut-style icons with transparent backgrounds. Organised by system role.

Corn Stover

04 — Gradient Fill System

Gradient Registers

Four fill registers drawn from the ecological color range — from luminous canopy light to deep field darkness and amber constraint.

Deep Field
Soil & Shadow
Use for hero moments, primary system anchors, and immersive callouts.
Canopy Light
Growth & Yield
Mimics sunlight filtering through a crop canopy. Use for enabling components and supporting context.
Radial Pulse · FARM-REC Core
Integrated System
Radial emergence from the system center. Reserved for the primary system node and key diagrams.
Amber Mesh · Constraint & Alert
Drivers & Constraints
Amber register signals complexity and constraint. Use for the Drivers panel and alert-level context.
05 — Landscape Scenes

Background Scenes

Four Midjourney-rendered atmospheric landscapes for slides, diagrams, and full-bleed backgrounds.

Scene 01Dairy Farms & Pasture
Livestock
Scene 02Clean Rivers with Cropland
Watershed
Scene 03Grain Storage Wheat Fields
Cropland
Scene 04Biochar Facility at Dawn
Pyrolysis
06 — Node Components

Diagram Nodes

Four node registers for system diagrams. Each pairs an icon with a role label, title, body copy, and optional chips.

Feedstock
Corn Stover
Post-harvest lignocellulosic biomass from corn production. High availability in Midwest systems.
LignocellulosicHigh Yield
Process
Microalgae Fermentation
Biological conversion of pretreated biomass using microalgae strains under controlled fermentation.
EnablingBiological
Primary Co-Product
Ruminant Feed Pellets
High-protein feed supplement for cattle and dairy operations. Primary revenue stream.
PrimaryHigh Value
Process
Pyrolysis
Thermochemical decomposition at 400–700°C under oxygen-limited conditions producing biochar, bio-oil, and syngas.
400–700°CThermochemical
System Context
Drivers & Constraints
Water scarcity · Input price volatility · Market instability · Environmental constraints · Producer decision-making.
WaterMarketsClimate
Co-Product
Biochar
Stable carbon-rich material for soil amendment and long-term carbon sequestration in agricultural land.
Soil AmendmentCarbon Sink

System Flow

Linear flow showing how drivers inform biomass inputs, which are processed and converted to co-products and system outcomes.

Outcomes
